values.value: Flags users whose API call volume in the last twenty-four hours exceeds twice their ninety-day trailing average, with a noise floor of one hundred calls per day. Sudden API spikes on a previously-quiet user often correlate with credential theft, runaway integration scripts, or unauthorized bulk extracts.

Identify the connected app or client driving the spike, confirm the integration owner authorized the change, and inspect IP and user-agent for the burst window. If unexplained, revoke the user's active sessions and OAuth tokens, throttle the integration user, and follow up with the connected-app owner.
